channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Republican lawsuits target rules for overseas voters, but those ballots are already sent
item.description: Trump and his Republican allies contend these ballots could be part of an elaborate scheme to steal the election from him, a claim for which there is no evidence. Their challenge comes as the voters who receive the ballots are increasingly from groups that are presumed to be Democratic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about shaken baby syndrome as a Texas man could be first in US executed over it
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man this week could become the first person executed in the U.S. from a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome. Robert Roberson, 57, is scheduled to receive a lethal injection on Thursday for the 2002 killing of his 2-year-old daughter, Nikki Curtis.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Country singer pauses concert as wife gives birth on tour bus
item.description: Country singer Brantley Gilbert's wife, Amber, welcomed their third child on a tour bus in the middle of his concert on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KREX 5

item.title: Kamala Harris will sit down with Bret Baier for her first Fox News interview
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris will be interviewed by Fox News anchor Bret Baier on Wednesday in Pennsylvania as she steps up her travel and conversations with media outlets in the closing stretch of the presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Disturbing video shows road rage suspect fatally shooting man in Los Angeles
item.description: Video obtained exclusively by KTLA shows the terrifying moment a road rage suspect opened fire on the driver of another car on the 5 Freeway in Los Angeles, fatally wounding him and seriously injuring a passenger.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5

item.title: Which are the most popular Halloween candies in Nevada?
item.description: Are you hoping the neighborhood mischief-makers won’t “T.P.” your house on Halloween? Then maybe you should hand out the candies they actually like.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florence nonprofit group brings animals to safety after hurricanes
item.description: A group teamed up with Southwest Airlines for an emergency airlift of shelter animals out of Myrtle Beach.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Huffstickler, NewsNation

item.title: 10 jurors seated so far in Delphi murders trial
item.description: The video above is from an earlier report ALLEN COUNTY, Ind. – Ten people have been chosen to sit on the jury in the Delphi murders trial after jury selection began Monday in Allen County.
item.dc:creator: Russ McQuaid, NewsNation

item.title: Bath and Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath and Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Senate rankings: 5 seats most likely to flip
item.description: The battle for the Senate majority has hit its final month as Republicans race to end their four-year stint in the minority. The GOP needs to win only one of two competitive races in red states — Montana and Ohio — to do just that, barring a surprise loss elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Pilot's wife safely lands in California after her husband suffers medical emergency during flight
item.description: The wife of a pilot who suffered a medical emergency during a flight took over flying and was able to safely land the aircraft in California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FEMA makes 'operational adjustments' following safety concerns in North Carolina
item.description: NORTH CAROLINA, N.C. (WGHP) — FEMA has made operational adjustments in North Carolina following safety concerns while providing relief in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene. A FEMA spokesperson told FOX8 the following.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, NewsNation

item.title: Jackson outduels Daniels and Henry runs for two TDs as Ravens beat Commanders 30-23
item.description: Lamar Jackson threw nine passes to Zay Flowers in the first half and never went to him again. Mark Andrews rebounded from a ball bouncing off his hands for an interception to catch his first touchdown pass of the season. And Derrick Henry was Derrick Henry.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump calls for protester to 'go back home to Mommy' during California rally
item.description: Trump called for the protester to “get the hell knocked out of her,” his latest instance of using violent language when confronted by demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What you should do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: Phones hold so much of our digital lives — emails, social media and bank accounts, photos, chat messages and more — that if they ever get stolen or go missing, it can cause major disruption
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fetterman unworried by tight Pennsylvania race
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman says Erie County, Pennsylvania, will pick the presidential winner of his state and the nation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: What is the purpose of an open-front toilet seat?
item.description: Open-front toilet seats — or “U-shaped” seats — are common in public bathrooms or commercial spaces, but they’re almost never the norm in private residences.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Biden views Hurricane Milton damage in Florida
item.description: President Joe Biden on Sunday will survey the devastation inflicted on Florida's Gulf Coast by Hurricane Milton as he urges Congress to approve additional emergency disaster funding.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Euthanasia, pronouns, sports betting: Intriguing 2024 ballot measures
item.description: Several states have placed important measures on their ballots, ranging from flag designs to end-of-life care.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Days left to claim cash in Oracle settlement that 'hundreds of millions' qualify for
item.description: The deadline is fast approaching for people to request a piece of the $115 million settlement fund Oracle agreed to pay out, settling claims it violated many people's privacy by selling their information to third parties.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Will Donald Trump and Kamala Harris debate again?
item.description: While there was originally talk of more than one meeting between Trump and Harris, the former president made his intentions clear with a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Prison operator under federal scrutiny spent millions settling Tennessee mistreatment claims
item.description: The leading private prison company in the U.S. has spent more than $4.4 million to settle dozens of complaints alleging mistreatment at its Tennessee prisons and jails since 2016.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Mattise, Travis Loller And Kristin M. Hall, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle school cheerleaders suffer first and second degree burns after 'punishment' from coach
item.description: Thirteen cheerleaders at Evans Middle School in Lubbock, Texas are suffering from first and second-degree burns on their hands after coach punishes team.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Washburn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What Scott Peterson's recent court win could mean for his yearslong quest for freedom
item.description: A California judge has granted convicted murderer Scott Peterson's request for discovery in his case after the Los Angeles Innocence Project took up his case in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida neighbors band together to recover after one-two punch from hurricanes Helene and Milton
item.description: The devastation of Hurricanes Helene and Milton is still being tallied as a swath of Florida comes to terms with damage from the unusual dual strike of storms in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Russ Bynum, Brendan Farrington And Ty Oneil, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Floridians cleaning up from Hurricane Milton are hampered by a widespread fuel shortage
item.description: CORTEZ, Fla. (AP) — Floridians recovering from Hurricane Milton, many of whom were journeying home after fleeing hundreds of miles to escape the storm, spent much of Saturday searching for gas as a fuel shortage gripped the state. In St.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY ONEIL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Asheville restaurant owners trying to recover from Helene
item.description: “Seeing the community come together like it has. It’s been incredibly heartwarming,” said Bryan King, co-owner of 12 Bones Smokehouse and Brewing.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KRON4

item.title: Rising chocolate prices impact Halloween candy choices
item.description: Despite rising chocolate and candy prices, Americans are expected to spend $3.5 million on Halloween sweets this year.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WGN 9

item.title: Thomas hits grand slam off Skubal, Guardians down Tigers 7-3 to reach AL Championship Series
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — For the third time in less than a month, the Cleveland Guardians made a semi-circle in the middle of their clubhouse and emptied champagne and beer bottles on each other as “Rocky Top” blasted through the speakers. The choice of music is unique. So is this team.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Far from where Hurricane Milton's eye hit, tornadoes wrought unexpected damage
item.description: WELLINGTON, Fla. (AP) — Tony Brazzale, a diving boat captain who has lived for 10 years in his Wellington home in southeastern Florida, wasn't worried about Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mud, beer and cash: Annual wife-carrying championship attracts competitive couples to Maine
item.description: More than 30 couples competed in the North American Wife Carrying Championship, a 278-yard (254-meter) race during which contestants splash through water, leap over logs and trudge through mud — all while carrying their partner like a sack of potatoes.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT F. BUKATY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Harris outpaces Trump in battleground Pennsylvania, but trails in Arizona: Poll
item.description: The New York Times/Philadelphia Inquirer/Siena College survey, released Saturday, shows Harris leading Trump 50 percent to 47 percent among likely voters in the Keystone State.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, ABC 4

item.title: Which states have the highest and lowest voter turnout in presidential elections?
item.description: The rate of voter turnout for the 2020 presidential election was around 67%. But some states were really bringing up that average.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Professor leaves KU after 'highly inappropriate' remarks during lecture
item.description: A University of Kansas professor has left the university after a video went viral on X showing the professor making unnerving remarks
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: How schools are tackling misinformation as false claims about hurricanes, election run wild
item.description: Misinformation generally has been an increasing concern, most recently with how the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) has been responding to the aftermath of Hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Hurricane Helene: More than 90 reported dead in North Carolina, authorities can’t yet confirm how many missing
item.description: Authorities in North Carolina have confirmed 94 storm-related fatalities from Hurricane Helene but many still remain missing or unaccounted for.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Polluted waste from Florida's fertilizer industry is in the path of Milton's fury
item.description: As Hurricane Milton pummeled Florida’s west coast with powerful winds and flooding rain, environmentalists worry it could scatter the polluted leftovers of the state’s phosphate fertilizer mining industry and other hazardous waste across the peninsula and into vulnerable waterways.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BIESECKER and JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, ABC 4
